## Preserved prior README notes

<!-- PRESERVED PRIOR README START -->
# extracted data

This folder is the public tabular-data layer for the PRT benchmark.
It is generated mechanically from `data_master_consolidation/` in the
source workspace.

## Layout

- `csv/`: the nine released consolidation CSVs, one per benchmark
  surface. These are copied from the live `final_*.csv` source files and
  renamed to stable public filenames.
- `schemas/`: column-level schema documents for the CSVs. These are
  copied from `raw_consolidations_data/MASTER_SCHEMAS/` and renamed from
  `*_MASTER_SCHEMA.md` to `*_schema.md`.
- `normalization/`: normalization guides, method dictionaries, and
  manual-review provenance ledgers.
- `DATA_DICTIONARY.md`: public overview of shared data conventions.

Answer-key files are intentionally not stored here. They live under
`../7.scoring/answer-key/`, and the scoring scripts in `../7.scoring/`
consume them to regenerate or verify verdict columns.

## Reproducibility Role

This folder is the input layer for both public scoring and public
analytics:

```text
6.extracted-data/csv/*.csv
  -> 7.scoring/add_*_answer_verdict_columns.py
  -> 7.scoring/pipeline_reports/*.json
  -> 8.analytics/*.py
  -> 8.analytics/*.md
```

If a reader wants to understand a field, start with `schemas/`. If a
reader wants to understand why a model response was normalized into a
method class or why a manual override was applied, start with
`normalization/`.
<!-- PRESERVED PRIOR README END -->
